instructions: DEMONSTRATION FIXTURE NOTE: This file uses Positive and Negative Affect Schedule (PANAS) content to demonstrate the assessment engine's two-dimension and pattern detection capabilities. It is NOT intended as a clinical or diagnostic tool and must not be used for clinical purposes. If you have concerns about your mental health or wellbeing, please speak with a qualified healthcare professional. -- Original instructions: This scale consists of a number of words that describe different feelings and emotions. Read each item and then indicate to what extent you have felt this way recently — over the past few weeks.

scaleLabels.1: Very slightly or not at all

Positive Affect.label: Positive Affect
Positive Affect.overview: Positive Affect reflects the extent to which a person feels enthusiastic, active, and alert. High PA is a state of high energy, full concentration, and pleasurable engagement.
Positive Affect.highMeaning: High scorers are experiencing frequent positive emotions such as enthusiasm, energy, and interest in the world around them.
Positive Affect.lowMeaning: Low scorers report feeling fatigued, subdued, or disengaged. Low PA is associated with reduced motivation and sadness.

Negative Affect.label: Negative Affect
Negative Affect.overview: Negative Affect is a general dimension of subjective distress encompassing a range of aversive mood states including nervousness, upset, and irritability.
Negative Affect.highMeaning: High scorers are experiencing frequent negative emotions such as anxiety, hostility, or distress.
Negative Affect.lowMeaning: Low scorers report feeling calm, composed, and relatively free from negative emotional experiences.

Positive Affect.High: You are experiencing strong positive engagement with life. Sustaining activities that energise and interest you will help maintain this.
Positive Affect.Moderate: You have a mixed positive affect profile. Consider which activities or relationships tend to lift your mood and energy.
Positive Affect.Low: You may be experiencing low energy or reduced motivation. Gentle activity, social connection, or speaking with someone you trust can help.

Negative Affect.High: You are experiencing elevated distress or negative emotion. It may be worth speaking with a trusted person or mental health professional.
Negative Affect.Moderate: You experience some negative emotions, which is normal. Noticing what triggers these feelings can be a useful first step.
Negative Affect.Low: You report low levels of distress and negative emotion, reflecting emotional calm and stability.

patterns.name: High Activation — Positive Engagement
patterns.description: High positive affect combined with low negative affect is the hallmark of a positive, energised emotional state. You are experiencing life with enthusiasm and relatively little distress — the most adaptive affect balance.

patterns.name: High Activation — Distressed
patterns.description: You are experiencing both high positive and high negative affect simultaneously. This pattern reflects a state of high emotional arousal in both directions — energised but also stressed. Sometimes associated with demanding but engaging circumstances.

patterns.name: Low Activation — Calm and Disengaged
patterns.description: Low scores on both dimensions indicate a calm but low-energy state. You are not experiencing significant distress, but also report little enthusiasm or engagement. This can reflect rest and recovery, or may indicate emotional flatness worth monitoring.

patterns.name: Low Activation — Negative
patterns.description: Low positive affect combined with high negative affect is associated with distress and emotional difficulty. You are experiencing significant negative emotions without the counterbalance of positive engagement. Speaking with a trusted person or professional is encouraged.
